---
title: "One-Way ANOVA in R"
author: "Dr Lindokuhle Dlamini"
output: html_document
---
## Download Dataset

<a href="data/One Way ANOVA Data.xlsx" download>Download the dataset used in this lesson</a>

---
# One-Way ANOVA in R

ANOVA is used to compare the means of **three or more groups**.

---

# Load Packages

```r
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggsignif)
```

---

# Import Data

```r
One_Way_ANOVA_Data <- read_excel("One Way ANOVA Data.xlsx")

View(One_Way_ANOVA_Data)

dataANOVA <- One_Way_ANOVA_Data

dataANOVA$Sites <- as.factor(dataANOVA$Sites)
```

---

# Run ANOVA

```r
ANOVA_RESULTS <- aov(`Plant richness` ~ Sites, data = dataANOVA)

anova(ANOVA_RESULTS)
```

---

# Test Normality of Residuals

```r
aov_residuals <- residuals(ANOVA_RESULTS)

shapiro.test(aov_residuals)
```

---

# Q-Q Plot

```r
plot(ANOVA_RESULTS,2)
```

---

# Tukey Post Hoc Test

```r
TukeyHSD(ANOVA_RESULTS)
```

---
